task: I need to send a short message about a schedule change to a client. Could you help me draft it so it’s clear and professional, with no extra fluff?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a short, professional draft:

**Hi [Client Name],**  
I wanted to let you know that there has been a slight change to our schedule. [Brief reason if needed]. The updated time is [new time/date]. Please let me know if you have any questions.  

If you want, I can also make it more formal or more casual.

user_simulator.text:
Pretty close, but it’s still a bit generic. I’d rather have something I can send as-is, with the placeholders filled in. Keep it short and plain. If you need a version, use this:

Hi [Client Name],

My schedule changed, so I need to move our appointment from [old time/date] to [new time/date]. Sorry for the inconvenience. Please let me know if that still works for you.

— [My Name]

turns.agent_response:
Hi [Client Name],

My schedule changed, so I need to move our appointment from [old time/date] to [new time/date]. Sorry for the inconvenience. Please let me know if that still works for you.

— [My Name]

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Short, plain, usable.
